core_mechanism: geometry-regularized optimal transport: solving for a cost-minimizing coupling that matches two distributions of features while a regularizer built from the Laplace-Beltrami spectral eigenbasis of the underlying manifold penalizes couplings that violate the domain's intrinsic geometry
brief_reason: The abstract describes aligning functional data across individuals (a transport/matching between two feature sets) with 'SpectralOT' embedding the domain's geometry into Laplace-Beltrami eigenmodes to regularize the alignment, i.e. an optimal-transport coupling regularized by a spectral-geometric prior.
